title: Ideology and economic policy
description: Test your skills on this quiz on ideology and economic policy
topic_path: How do political beliefs shape views on the economy?

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is an accurate comparison of typical conservative and liberal views concerning economic policy? **

 | Liberal | Conservative
- | - | -
**A** | The Federal Reserve System should be solely responsible for stabilizing the economy | Congress should have a shared responsibility with the Federal Reserve System for stabilizing the economy
**B** | Cutting taxes helps to stimulate the economy by increasing the supply of goods | Cutting spending to entitlement programs like social security helps to stimulate the economy 
**C** | Increasing spending on public works projects can provide much needed jobs for the unemployed | Raising taxes on top earners will give the government more money to redistribute to the people who need it most
**D** | The federal government should have a stronger role in regulating the economy | The federal government should have a less involved role in regulating the economy

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Comparison A
Comparison B
Comparison C
Comparison D

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is an accurate comparison of typical conservative and liberal views concerning economic policy?**

 | Liberal | Conservative
- | - | -
**A** | The federal government should have a less involved role in regulating the economy | The federal government should have a stronger role in regulating the economy
**B** | Raising taxes on top US earners allows the government to help the middle class | Cutting federal regulations and taxes provides businesses with more opportunities to hire more workers
**C** | Cutting spending on entitlement programs helps to stimulate the economy | Decreasing government spending can help to stimulate the economy 
**D** | When unemployment is high, the government should not intervene | When unemployment is high, the government should create jobs to decrease unemployment

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Comparison A
Comparison B
Comparison C
Comparison D

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/915bae12e1d1f333e213a52650c1cbc3.png)

>Source: Boris Rasin, [Flickr](https://www.flickr.com/photos/58551403@N06/6127944739/)

**Which of the following ideological perspectives would the “fat cat” in this cartoon most likely have?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Conservative
Liberal
Communitarian
Progressive

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/915bae12e1d1f333e213a52650c1cbc3.png)

>Source: Boris Rasin, [Flickr](https://www.flickr.com/photos/58551403@N06/6127944739/)

**Which of the following best describes the message in the political cartoon?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Supply-side economic policies are controversial when they cut taxes for the rich, because they may not use that money to expand the American economy
Supply-side economic policies are successful because tax cuts for top earners allow those earners to create more jobs
Keynesian economic policies are controversial because the government takes too active a role in regulating the economy
Keynesian economic policies are successful because the government can react to the natural ups and downs of the economy

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following measures would a president adhering to supply-side economic principles most likely employ during an economic recession?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A national budget with significant cuts in federal spending on entitlement programs like Medicaid
A national budget that increases money sent to state governments to fix infrastructure problems like filling potholes and finishing highways
An executive order to increase the number of dollars in circulation in the economy
Legislation that creates a new jobs training program for those who are currently unemployed

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following measures would a president adhering to supply-side economic principles most likely employ during an economic recession?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A budget that cuts individual and corporate income tax and decreases government regulation of the economy
A budget that raises taxes on the top earners so that the government can redistribute the funds to the bottom 25% of earners
A budget with significant increases in federal spending on entitlement programs like Medicaid
A budget that increases funds for physics and science research for the National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following measures would a president adhering to Keynesian economic principles most likely employ during an economic recession?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: An executive order that built three solar panel farms and two wind-powered generators in rural areas to decrease unemployment
A national budget with dramatic cuts in federal spending for the education department and defense department
An executive order to reduce the federal interest rate from 1.65% to 1.0%
Legislation that eliminates Environmental Protection Agency regulations to reduce the cost of operation for major businesses

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following measures would a president adhering to Keynesian economic principles most likely employ during an economic recession?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A budget with significant additions of public work projects to provide employment for Americans without jobs
A budget with significant cuts in federal spending on entitlement programs like Medicaid
A budget that raises the federal interest rate from 1.65% to 1.8%
A budget that cuts corporate income tax rates and decrease regulation of the economy

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is an accurate comparison of monetary policy and fiscal policy?**

 | Monetary policy | Fiscal policy
- | - | -
**A** | Using taxes and government spending to stabilize the economy  | Making changes in the money supply or the interest rate to stabilize the economy
**B** | Changing the discount rate to address inflationary pressure in the economy | Changing how many dollars are produced after the economy fails
**C** | Controlled by central banks, like the Federal Reserve System | Controlled by Congress and the president
**D** | Affected by partisan views on the economy | Insulated from partisan views on the economy

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Comparison A
Comparison B
Comparison C
Comparison D

assessment_items.question_content:
**If Congress adheres to a liberal economic policy, which of the following measures would it most likely employ during an economic recession?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Legislation to increase corporate income tax rates and increase regulation of the economy
Legislation allowing Americans to engage in a barter system for goods without paying taxes
A constitutional amendment that prohibits the federal government from creating government subsidies and bailouts to businesses
A constitutional amendment that repeals the income tax

assessment_items.question_content:
**If Congress adheres to a conservative economic policy, which of the following measures would it most likely employ during an economic recession?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Legislation to reduce regulations on the operation of small businesses
Legislation to increase corporate income tax rates and increase regulation of the economy
A constitutional amendment that creates a federal tax on assets like homes and cash after a person dies
A constitutional amendment that progressively increases income taxes for the top 10% of earners in the United States

assessment_items.question_content:
**If Congress adheres to a libertarian economic policy, which of the following measures would it most likely employ during an economic recession?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A constitutional amendment that prohibits the federal government from creating government subsidies and bailouts to businesses
A constitutional amendment that would increases income taxes for the top 10% of earners in the United States
Legislation to make the minimum wage in all states \$15 an hour to keep pace with the rising cost of living
Legislation to increase the power of the Environmental Protection Agency to prosecute companies that violate clean energy regulations

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is an accurate comparison of fiscal policy and monetary policy in the United States?**

|   | **Fiscal Policy**                                       | **Monetary Policy**                                              |
|---|--------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **A** | Controlled by the Federal Reserve  | Controlled by Congress and the president     |
| **B** | Focuses on setting interest rates to influence economic growth and borrowing  | Involves adjusting tax rates and government spending  |
| **C** | Enacted by Congress and the president through the budget  | Implemented by the Federal Reserve to manage the money supply |
| **D** | Operates independently of elected officials   | Requires direct approval from Congress for all changes  |

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Comparison A
Comparison B
Comparison C
Comparison D

assessment_items.question_content:
Unemployment claims are rising and consumer spending is slowing, signaling a potential economic downturn. Businesses are cutting back on hiring, and fears of a recession are growing. In response, the Federal Reserve announces that it will lower interest rates. 

**What is the Federal Reserve’s likely goal in lowering interest rates?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To stimulate the economy by encouraging borrowing and investment
To reduce inflation by tightening control over the supply of the U.S. dollar
To raise the value of the U.S. dollar in global currency markets
To reduce demand by slowing wage growth and consumer spending

assessment_items.question_content:
The U.S. economy is slowing down. Unemployment is rising, businesses are reporting lower revenues, and spending is declining. In response, Congress passes a stimulus package that includes direct payments to individuals and expanded unemployment benefits. Lawmakers say they hope the policy will have an impact on the economy.

**What is the intended short-term economic effect of this policy?**



[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Consumer demand is likely to rise, which could lead to increased employment.
The value of the U.S. dollar is likely to increase due to a reduction in government debt.
Prices are likely to fall as consumer spending decreases.
The Federal Reserve will likely be required to raise interest rates in order to prevent inflation.
